text:
# Chief_Complaint
- Follow-up for diabetes and hypertension management
# HPI
The patient presents for a follow-up visit regarding their diabetes and hypertension management.
They report that their blood sugar has improved since starting Farxiga, which was recently approved by their insurance with a $15 copay.
However, the patient states that their blood sugar is "not perfect" and requires careful monitoring.
They also mention difficulty controlling their anger when blood sugar levels fluctuate.
Regarding hypertension, the patient reports taking glimepiride twice daily and metformin twice daily.
They are also taking benazepril 40 mg twice daily for blood pressure management.
The patient notes that their evening blood sugar levels are typically low, around 70 mg/dL, while morning levels can be as high as 150 mg/dL.
They take their evening medication around 5:00 PM, sometimes later if they forget.
The patient reports recent improvements in mobility but still experiences shortness of breath with exertion.
They mention that their back "locked up" for three days after doing some work yesterday.
The patient confirms they receive an annual flu shot in the fall and are due for a pneumonia booster at age 65.
The patient uses a CPAP machine for sleep apnea and reports purchasing a new mask recently.
They also use albuterol and Symbicort inhalers, primarily when going outside.
The patient is on lasix for fluid management but is currently not taking it due to perceived overlap with Farxiga's diuretic effects.
The patient mentions they are on their "last refill" from their gastroenterologist and plan to schedule a follow-up appointment soon.
They have been using fiber and stool softeners as recommended by their gastroenterologist.
# ROS
## General
Reports polyuria.
## Musculoskeletal
Reports back spasms/contracture.
## Psychiatric
Reports anger issues.
# Past_History_Medical
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus, managed with Farxiga, glimepiride, and metformin.
- Hypertension, managed with benazepril and metoprolol.
- Hyperlipidemia, managed with lovastatin.
- Chronic back pain.
- Obstructive sleep apnea, managed with CPAP.
- Chronic respiratory condition, managed with albuterol nebulizer and Symbicort inhaler.
- History of gastrointestinal issues, managed with fiber and stool softeners.
# Past_History_Surgical
# Past_History_Social
Patient drives frequently for long distances.
Has a daughter who provides support.
# Past_History_Family
# Allergies
# Medications
- Farxiga
- Glimepiride, twice daily
- Metformin, twice daily
- Benazepril 40 mg, twice daily
- Metoprolol
- Lasix
- Lovastatin
- Verapamil
- Albuterol nebulizer
- Symbicort inhaler
- Fiber supplement
- Stool softener
# Diagnostic_Testing
- Hemoglobin A1c level is 7.6%, which, while an improvement, is still not at goal.
- Lipid panel results are within normal limits.
- Renal function is within normal limits.
# Physical_Exam
## Cardiovascular
Heart sounds auscultated.
## Respiratory
Lung sounds auscultated.
# Assessment_Plan
## Diabetes mellitus, uncontrolled
- Continue Farxiga, glimepiride, and metformin.
- Adjusted the timing of glimepiride and metformin administration to later in the evening or at bedtime to help mitigate the morning hyperglycemia and evening hypoglycemia.
- Instructed patient to monitor blood glucose levels more closely, especially in the morning and evening, to identify patterns and help with dose timing adjustments.
- Encouraged patient to continue monitoring blood pressure, as it is a lifelong condition that requires vigilance.
- Refilled prescription for 90 days (where allowed) for most medications to align with patient's preference for fewer pharmacy visits.
- Deferred lipid panel at next visit due to previous normal results.
- Scheduled follow-up visit in 3 months to assess hemoglobin A1c, renal function, and medication efficacy.
## Hypertension
- Continue benazepril and metoprolol.
- Increased the dosage of metoprolol to twice daily to optimize blood pressure control.
- Refilled prescription for 90 days (where allowed) for most medications to align with patient's preference for fewer pharmacy visits.
- Deferred lipid panel at next visit due to previous normal results.
- Scheduled follow-up visit in 3 months to assess hemoglobin A1c, renal function, and medication efficacy.
## Hyperlipidemia
- Continue lovastatin.
- Deferred lipid panel at next visit due to previous normal results.
- Scheduled follow-up visit in 3 months to assess hemoglobin A1c, renal function, and medication efficacy.
## Other
- Administered influenza vaccination today in clinic.
- Patient is due for pneumococcal vaccine booster at age 65.
